title: Enable EKS Auto Mode with the correct NodeClass reference
scenario: Your team wants zero node management overhead. EKS Auto Mode handles the data plane — AMI updates, node scaling, CNI, kube-proxy, CoreDNS, and the load balancer controller — all managed by AWS. You need to create a Karpenter NodePool that references EKS Auto Mode's built-in `NodeClass`. The starter NodePool references `wrong-class` instead of the required `default` NodeClass.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: karpenter.sh/v1
kind: NodePool
metadata:
  name: auto-mode-pool
spec:
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        eks-auto: "true"
    spec:
      nodeClassRef:
        group: eks.amazonaws.com
        kind: NodeClass
        name: wrong-class
      requirements:
      - key: karpenter.sh/capacity-type
        operator: In
        values: ["on-demand"]
  disruption:
    consolidationPolicy: WhenEmpty
    consolidateAfter: 1h

hints.text: EKS Auto Mode provides a single built-in NodeClass named `default` in the `eks.amazonaws.com` API group. Any other name will fail to provision nodes.
Change `nodeClassRef.name: wrong-class` to `nodeClassRef.name: default`. The group and kind are already correct.

The complete nodeClassRef for EKS Auto Mode:
```yaml
nodeClassRef:
  group: eks.amazonaws.com
  kind: NodeClass
  name: default
```

solution:
apiVersion: karpenter.sh/v1
kind: NodePool
metadata:
  name: auto-mode-pool
spec:
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        eks-auto: "true"
    spec:
      nodeClassRef:
        group: eks.amazonaws.com
        kind: NodeClass
        name: default
      requirements:
      - key: karpenter.sh/capacity-type
        operator: In
        values: ["on-demand"]
  disruption:
    consolidationPolicy: WhenEmpty
    consolidateAfter: 1h
